immutable_record_notice: Once frozen, this study manifest is immutable. Changes require a new record version linked to the superseded raw-byte digest.

amendment.rationale: Initial draft prepared for independent review and a separate authoritative freeze action.

subject.name: Candidate Formal Memory Object Model v0.1
subject.definition: A product-independent candidate formal memory object model comprising typed entities, relations, lifecycle operations, temporal and authority semantics, candidate invariants, intended non-entailments, countermodels, and open proof obligations.
subject.boundary: Registry presence fixes the exact candidate bytes for future Layer 1 formal analysis. It is not a proof of consistency, satisfiability, soundness, or realizability, and it does not establish adoption, scientific evidence, mechanism validation, security assurance, or implementation authorization.

assumptions: FMO-A01-EXACT-SUBJECT: analyze only the exact registered subject bytes.
FMO-A02-CLASSICAL-BOOLEAN-KERNEL: use two-valued classical truth conditions.
FMO-A03-MODULE-LOCALITY: do not infer a cross-module full model.
FMO-A05-FINITE-BOUNDS: exhaustiveness applies only inside declared modules.
FMO-A07-NONENTAILMENT-SCOPE: require a retained premise-true conclusion-false witness.
FMO-A08-INDEPENDENCE-SCOPE: omit exactly one invariant within its module.
FMO-A09-AUTHORITY-ABSTRACTION: authority decisions are opaque observations, not a policy calculus.
FMO-A10-DELETION-ABSTRACTION: closure and non-reconstruction facts are declared inputs, not physical observations.
FMO-A11-DETERMINISTIC-OFFLINE-EXECUTION: use the exact offline standard-library toolchain.
FMO-A12-NO-RESULT-ACCESS-BEFORE-FREEZE: forbid creating or inspecting planned outputs before authoritative freeze.
consistency_or_satisfiability_checks: Exhaustively enumerate every Boolean valuation of each enumerated module and retain its exact valuation count, satisfying-model count, and first satisfying model.
Reject bounded module satisfiability when any enumerated module has zero models; invalidate incomplete or duplicate enumeration.
Do not infer that independently satisfying module models compose into one full FMO-0.1 structure.
intended_entailments: Search FMO-P01 through FMO-P04 and FMO-IE-01 through FMO-IE-10 for satisfying premise-true conclusion-false valuations.
Retain every violating valuation and reject the affected bounded entailment; treat an unsatisfiable premise as inconclusive rather than favorable.
non_entailments_or_countermodels: Retain a satisfying premise-true conclusion-false assignment for every FMO-NE-01 through FMO-NE-24 query, including separate FMO-NE-12A and FMO-NE-12B consequents.
Evaluate and retain the complete frozen assignments for FMO-CM-01 through FMO-CM-10.
A witness establishes freedom only within the bounded encoding and never proves a real-world or full-FMO countermodel.
primary_method_profile.proof_or_verification_method: After authoritative freeze only, use the exact offline CPython 3.12.13 standard-library analyzer to exhaustively enumerate the declared finite Boolean modules, retain models/counterexamples/witnesses, and perform one-at-a-time invariant omission. Then complete an independent human semantic-validity adjudication. Parsing, schema validity, compilation, or execution success alone is insufficient.
primary_method_profile.semantic_validity_check: Two reviewers independently map every decision-bearing variable, constraint, premise, conclusion, witness, and scope exclusion to exact FMO-0.1 text and classify it as faithful, narrower, broader, circular, ambiguous, or unsupported. Broader, circular, or unsupported mappings invalidate affected analyses; unresolved ambiguity makes them inconclusive.
primary_method_profile.counterexample_search: Enumerate all variables relevant to each query plus all variables used by its containing constraints, preserving the first complete satisfying witness in deterministic order. Enumerate every variable for module satisfiability. Search completeness is limited to the frozen finite kernel and does not extend to unbounded FMO-0.1 domains or histories.

research_question: Within an explicitly bounded, product-independent Boolean encoding of the exact registered FMO-0.1 subject, do the selected invariant constraints admit module-local models; do encoded intended entailments resist exhaustive counterexample search; do explicit non-entailments and named countermodels retain witnesses; and which invariant, authority, and deletion claims fail independence or semantic-validity review?

hypothesis.claim: Every enumerated frozen kernel module admits a satisfying valuation; encoded intended entailments have no satisfying counterexample; every encoded non-entailment and FMO-CM-01 through FMO-CM-10 retains a witness; and all decision-bearing mappings are independently adjudicated faithful or narrower.
hypothesis.competing_explanation: Constraints may merely restate conclusions, omit material FMO semantics, or form independently satisfying modules that cannot combine, so favorable finite results may reflect encoding weakness rather than a defensible property of FMO-0.1.
hypothesis.predicted_observation: Complete retained valuation counts and models for every enumerated module, no violating valuation for each intended entailment, a full witness for every non-entailment and named countermodel, one omission disposition per invariant, and two retained semantic mappings plus adjudication for every decision-bearing encoding element.
hypothesis.falsifier: Any zero-model module, intended-entailment counterexample, absent non-entailment or named-countermodel witness, prohibited authority/deletion valuation, incomplete enumeration, failed frozen provenance binding, or broader/circular/unsupported decision-bearing mapping rejects, invalidates, or materially narrows the claim.

analyses.question: Does every enumerated frozen Boolean module admit at least one satisfying valuation under complete module-local enumeration?
analyses.method: Enumerate exactly 2^n valuations for each n-variable enumerated module, apply every frozen module constraint, retain the count and first model, and separately check that each entailment premise is satisfiable.
analyses.decision_rule: COMPLETED with a bounded positive observation only if every module has complete coverage and at least one model. A zero-model module rejects bounded satisfiability; an unsatisfied query premise is inconclusive for that entailment; missing or duplicate valuations invalidate; an integrity stop halts.

analyses.question: Do FMO-P01 through FMO-P04 and FMO-IE-01 through FMO-IE-10 resist exhaustive bounded counterexample search?
analyses.method: For each frozen entailment, search satisfying valuations for premise=true and conclusion=false, retain the first counterexample, and preserve premise satisfiability and module scope.
analyses.decision_rule: A valid counterexample rejects that encoded entailment. No counterexample supports only a within-kernel observation when the premise is satisfiable and semantic review passes. Vacuity is inconclusive; incomplete enumeration or semantic mismatch invalidates.

analyses.question: Does every frozen explicit non-entailment and FMO-CM-01 through FMO-CM-10 retain a valid bounded witness?
analyses.method: Search for a satisfying premise-true conclusion-false assignment for each FMO-NE query and evaluate each exact named countermodel assignment against its module constraints and witness condition.
analyses.decision_rule: A missing or invalid witness rejects the affected bounded non-entailment claim. Complete witnesses are positive only within the kernel and only after semantic review; missing coverage or semantic mismatch invalidates.

analyses.question: Which FMO-INV-01 through FMO-INV-16 constraints admit a one-at-a-time module-local independence witness?
analyses.method: Remove one invariant constraint, preserve every remaining constraint in its module, and retain a valuation that violates the omitted formula if one exists.
analyses.decision_rule: A witness establishes independence only within that module. No witness is NOT-DEMONSTRATED and triggers redundancy, contradiction, joint-dependence, or bound follow-up; it is not automatic rejection. Missing targets or incomplete search invalidate.

analyses.question: Does the bounded authority surface forbid state-changing success under DENY or UNRESOLVED and preserve role-without-universal-authority?
analyses.method: Jointly interpret FMO-INV-05, FMO-IE-05, FMO-NE-22, the FMO-INV-05 omission probe, and FMO-CM-08 under the frozen module and semantic mapping.
analyses.decision_rule: Any satisfying prohibited state-change valuation or missing role-without-universal-authority witness rejects the bounded claim. No result composes policy, establishes legitimacy, or proves liveness; semantic mismatch invalidates.

analyses.question: Do the bounded deletion constraints prevent verified labels under incomplete scope and prevent resurrection of the same exact identity?
analyses.method: Jointly interpret FMO-INV-14 through INV-16, FMO-P03, FMO-IE-07/10, FMO-NE-16/18/19, FMO-CM-04/06/10, invariant omissions, and the frozen unknown/access/reconstruction/local-erasure boundary assignments.
analyses.decision_rule: Any model allowing verified success with unknown, accessible, reconstructable, incomplete, or local-erasure-only facts, or same-exact-identity resurrection, rejects the bounded claim. No physical, external, legal, or operational deletion conclusion is permitted.

analyses.question: Are all decision-bearing source-to-encoding mappings faithful or narrower and non-circular?
analyses.method: Two reviewers independently map every decision-bearing variable, constraint, premise, conclusion, witness, and exclusion to exact subject text, retain their classifications and disagreement, and adjudicate without deleting originals.
analyses.decision_rule: Any unremedied BROADER, CIRCULAR, or UNSUPPORTED mapping invalidates affected analyses. Unresolved AMBIGUOUS mapping makes them inconclusive. Missing independent mapping or adjudication invalidates this analysis.

reproducibility.source_revision: Author package merge 9966c31a86c999c5eda53aced8bb4274f2cbdc2d; canonical freeze integration exact-head reviewed on 2026-07-21.
replication_criteria: Resolve the same active frozen study record, exact subject/profile/formal-system identities, and all raw-byte input digests.

Enumerate the same relevant valuations with no omission or duplication and agree exactly on model counts, counterexample existence, lexicographically first witnesses, and named-countermodel predicates.
Retain all raw outputs, deviations, negative/null/invalid outcomes, and a new independent semantic-validity mapping.
Disclose runtime or implementation differences and demonstrate truth-condition equivalence; no numerical tolerance applies.

rejection_criteria.condition: Any enumerated module has zero satisfying valuations under exact frozen constraints.
rejection_criteria.rationale: Rejects bounded module satisfiability and may expose contradiction or encoding defect.

rejection_criteria.condition: A satisfying premise-true conclusion-false assignment exists for an intended entailment.
rejection_criteria.rationale: Direct bounded counterexample to the encoded entailment.

rejection_criteria.condition: No valid witness exists for a frozen non-entailment or named countermodel.
rejection_criteria.rationale: The bounded encoding does not admit the declared separation.

rejection_criteria.condition: A decision-bearing mapping is broader, circular, or unsupported.
rejection_criteria.rationale: Machine semantics cannot support the source claim.

rejection_criteria.condition: An intended-entailment premise has no satisfying model.
rejection_criteria.rationale: Absence of a counterexample would be vacuous.

rejection_criteria.condition: DENY or UNRESOLVED coexists with successful state change, or an actor role entails universal authority.
rejection_criteria.rationale: Violates the frozen operation-scoped authority boundary.

rejection_criteria.condition: Verified deletion coexists with unknown, accessible, reconstructable, incomplete, or local-erasure-only in-scope facts.
A terminal deleted exact identity becomes later accessible, non-deleted, or reused.
Any exact identity, version, digest, freeze authority, canonical record-index binding, input, or environment binding fails.
rejection_criteria.rationale: Results would not bind to the frozen study.

rejection_criteria.condition: Any planned output exists or was inspected before authoritative freeze.
rejection_criteria.rationale: Confirmatory classification is compromised and requires supersession.

rejection_criteria.condition: Any required valuation, query, invariant, or countermodel is missing, duplicated, or silently coerced.
Execution uses a network, model, external solver, dependency, mutable source, or path outside the repository beyond frozen provenance.
rejection_criteria.rationale: The method or security boundary changed after freeze.

rejection_criteria.condition: Any output is characterized as proving or validating all FMO-0.1.
rejection_criteria.rationale: Exceeds the formal-system and evidence boundary.

ethics_scope.rationale: The study uses only public, non-human formal specifications and deterministic synthetic Boolean structures.

assets: Exact subject, study, profile, formal-system, protocol, freeze, tool, configuration, and result provenance.
Complete valuation coverage, raw outputs, independent semantic mappings, disagreements, and negative or invalid dispositions.
threats: Subject or CLI-input substitution, forged digests or freeze authority, path traversal, execution before freeze, output overwrite, hidden analyzer change, valuation omission, semantic overclaim, reviewer capture, and undeclared service or dependency use.
False authority or deletion conclusions caused by opaque policy facts, incomplete closure, or treating abstract variables as operational observations.
controls: Canonical record-index digest binding, root-contained exact paths, immutable raw-byte SHA-256 references, exact IDs and versions, frozen execution guard, exclusive-create output, offline standard-library execution, expression whitelist, complete retained assignments, independent semantic review, and immutable dispositions.
Explicit denial of policy legitimacy, physical deletion, external-copy, implementation, and full-FMO conclusions.
security_and_privacy_analysis.data_retention_and_deletion: Retain the frozen record, freeze artifact, all exact inputs, analyzer, environment/configuration, raw outputs, digests, reviewer mappings, disagreement, adjudication, deviations, and every terminal disposition as program research records. No personal or participant data is expected. If sensitive material is accidentally introduced, halt and follow a separately authorized incident and deletion process without silently rewriting frozen history.
residual_risks: Digest and repository controls do not establish semantic fidelity, toolchain integrity, or reviewer independence.
The bounded Boolean abstraction can hide contradictions, cross-module incompatibility, policy complexity, reconstruction channels, and information leakage.

limitations: The finite modules do not establish one combined, nontrivial model of all FMO-0.1.
Many constraints encode desired consequences directly and require anti-circularity review.
The Python enumerator is a study-local tool, not an independently verified proof assistant or model checker.
Natural-language, quantified, temporal, distributed, probabilistic, policy-composition, liveness, and information-flow semantics are omitted.
Authority decisions are opaque; no legitimacy, jurisdiction, delegation, revocation, obligation, or liveness conclusion is available.
Deletion closure, materialization, and reconstruction are input facts; no physical, external, legal, or operational deletion is established.
One-at-a-time module omission can miss joint and cross-module invariant dependence.
Exact replication can reproduce the same encoding error; semantic review remains judgment-dependent.
No human-subject, controlled empirical, operational, security-penetration, latency, storage, compute, or token-cost evaluation occurs.

mechanism_maturity_boundary.rationale: This is mechanism-free Layer 1 analysis of a candidate formal subject. Registration, parsing, satisfiability, entailment, countermodels, semantic review, findings, or closeout cannot award or change a Charter mechanism evidence label.
